programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight we meet people working hard to bring New England treasures back to life, from Grandma’s old rocking chair to a century-old pipe organ. In Dartmouth, Ted Reinstein meets a brass finisher who makes “cruddy stuff” gleam again – including the foghorn from the sunken ship, Andrea Doria. Anthony Everett steps inside a Boston church where Tiffany-designed stained glass windows are shedding centuries of grime to shine once more. And in Lowell, efforts are underway to restore a Skinner organ to its musical glory.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight we bring you a buffet of breakfast stories, including a farm-to-table restaurant serving breakfast with homegrown ingredients, and a funky beachside cafe on Plum Island.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Stop and smell the Roses, and meet the people who grow them.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: Always Remember Your Name
programme.desc: A documentary of mistaken identity, hope, love, and the power of story by two Holocaust survivors.

programme.title: WCVB 50 Years Special
programme.desc: (2022) WCVB Channel 5 celebrates 50 years - from the very first moments on the air to meeting the people who make it all happen.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Many of the towns of Boston’s North Shore are thought of simply as bedroom communities. However, they are more than just homes for people who work in the Hub. Beverly, Salem, Ipswich and Swampscott have rich maritime histories and traditions still alive today. Tonight, Ted Reinstein travels the Main Streets and Back Roads around Route 1A North.

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: It’s not New Hampshire’s oldest or longest highway, but it is one of its most storied. Tonight, Ted Reinstein makes a return trip to Route 4, including the stretch known as “Antique Alley” where he finds vintage records (and a very large stuffed shark). Ted’s trip continues east where he paddles through a salt marsh found, improbably, 20 miles inland from the Seacoast. He fuels up at the classic Gilley’s Diner in Portsmouth before touring the historic and moving sites along Portsmouth’s Black Heritage Trail.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Since the Earl of Sandwich accidentally created them, it seems New Englanders have been working to perfect the art of the sandwich. Tonight we embark on a mouthwatering sandwich safari

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: Wellness Hour or Get Fixed

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: It’s the season of giving, but tonight we meet the people giving gifts from the heart – all year round. Erika Tarantal dons fairy wings to deliver goodies to sick children through the Happy Hope Factory in Bourne. At Runway to Recovery, Shayna Seymour meets a Newburyport woman bringing hope and a sense of normalcy to families with loved ones who are battling cancer. We also chat with Massachusetts First Lady Lauren Baker, who leads the Wonderfund, supporting children who are engaged with the foster care system. Finally, we meet seniors seeing their wishes come true – and young students being given the gift of music.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: If it kills you to throw anything away, find inspiration in our stories tonight. We trace what happens after people leave unwanted clothing in a collection bin, and meet an entrepreneur who turns old skis and snowboards into seating. At The Great Exchange in Devens, nonprofits shop for office furniture and other leftovers donated by area businesses. And secondhand stores aren’t just for clothing and furniture: Make & Mend offers gently used arts and crafts supplies.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news and weather from WCVB NewsCenter 5.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: On April Fools’ Day we’ll have you seeing double as we head out in search of doubles and doppelgangers.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: The world is watching as millions of displaced Ukrainians struggle to find safety. If you were suddenly one of the 26 million refugees around the world, what would you do? Tonight Nicole Estaphan shares stories of refugees from Ukraine, Afghanistan, and Syria, who are beginning new lives in Massachusetts and beyond. In Methuen, we meet an Afghan journalist starting from scratch with his wife and young daughter. Nicole visits a Newburyport church that houses refugee families, and talks with the Boston-based non-profit Beyond Conflict. Finally, Erika Tarantal brings us the story of one refugee who made it from war-torn Bosnia to Brookline, where she now owns a small business.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: We’ve all heard of the Coney Island hot dog eating contest, but Shayna Seymour and Erika Tarantal are curious: What about food competitions right here in New England?

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Shayna Seymour's episode picks of 2023.

programme.desc: Wellness Hour or Get Fixed

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Want to shake off the December chill? Tonight, Shayna Seymour takes us back to the sunny island of Bermuda as she looks back on her favorite stories of 2025.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Martha’s Vineyard is Shayna’s “happy place.” She spends every summer on the island, but this year, she had a chance to visit during springtime. Back on the mainland, she met a young man who turned his love of comic books into a business. Also this year, she had a chance to “pixel” herself (you have to watch to understand). Tonight, a look back at Shayna’s favorite stories of 2021.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: Nestled in a bend of the Deerfield River with its famous Bridge of Flowers, classic Main Street, and dramatic waterfall, Shelburne Falls is right out of a movie set. It is no wonder *three* major productions have come to town in recent years. As Anthony Everett points out, Shelburne Falls is the scene for movie make-believe because, technically, Shelburne Falls is not a real town at all. Tonight, the wonder and beauty of this slice of Western Massachusetts.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: In Shelburne we find local charm, fascinating history, a classic general store, and a bevy of (teddy) bears – all wrapped in a blanket of gorgeous fall color. The 1,400-acre Shelburne Farms is famed for its Vermont cheddar – and gorgeous grounds laid out by Frederick Law Olmsted. The Shelburne Museum is jam-packed with art and oddities. We meet a well-known musician whose side hustle is highlighting “weirdness.” And no trip to Shelburne is complete without a visit to the Vermont Teddy Bear Company.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight we take a walk in the shoes of New England shoe designers, collectors, and makers. We also discover how a bar mitzvah project grew into a national movement, Gotta Have Sole, to provide new sneakers for kids experiencing homelessness.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Just 10 shopping days left before Christmas! Tonight we bring you stories of local makers and entrepreneurs who are meeting customers where they live. We meet a couple crafting unique glass products on the North Shore, and discover a gift box service that specializes in local products. On Cape Cod, Nicole Estaphan visits the Christmas shop at Cape Abilities for gifts that give back – local products that benefit the nonprofit’s clients. Finally, you might expect to find only major national chains at the Burlington Mall – but we talk to local mom-and-pop vendors getting their mall moment, too.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: It’s gift-giving season, and tonight we bring you stories of local makers and entrepreneurs. We visit a glittering, nature-inspired shop in Lynn, owned by a husband-and-wife team.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: Wellness Hour or Get Fixed

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: If you’re looking for a sign, tonight we have plenty! In Somerville, Sav-Mor Liquors has been serving up snickers on its cheeky outdoor sign for more than a decade. We visit an old-school neon sign shop, meet the couple behind a throwback sign-painting company, and chat with a restaurant server turned professional chalk artist. And it wouldn’t be a show about signs without a look at Boston’s most iconic – the Citgo sign over Kenmore Square.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight, we’re off to ski towns, some just a few gallons of gas away. First, Anthony Everett heads to the Nashoba Valley Ski Area and explores the town of Westford (including Winnie the Pooh’s “house”) and an indoor butterfly garden; Shayna Seymour goes tubing at Butternut Ski Area in Great Barrington, then stops for some French pastries and checks in to at local bed and breakfast; and Erika Tarantal heads to Shrewsbury where she hits the slopes (and is put to work) at Ski Ward, grabs a bite to eat at a local sushi shop, and fuels up with some sugar at a local candy shop.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the huge eyes of the dark-dwelling owl and giant squid.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Learn from the experts on what do with ‘old stuff,’ when to keep, gift or throw away.

programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: Best of Chronicle: New England Eats
programme.desc: In honor of National Dessert Day we’re diving into the Chronicle vault for our sugar fix! Are you a cake person or a pie person? Tonight you don’t have to choose as we revisit a specialty cake shop in Brookline and a pie shop in Somerville. We sample classic Italian pastries in Lawrence, and do a deep dive on everyone’s favorite sweet – chocolate.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: As winter in New England drags on we can use a little sparkle. Tonight, all things shiny: From the literal sparkle of gold at the Harvard Mineralogical and Geological Museum, to a night out in Cambridge with The Glitter Boys, who spin vinyl from the ‘70s and ‘80s.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: WCVB Local News
programme.desc: Get the latest news and weather from WCVB.

programme.title: Fighting for Freedom: The United States v. Hamid Hayat
programme.desc: 14 years after he was arrested on federal terrorism charges, a Northern California man is freed after his case is overturned.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Nails and knives…bears and ballet slippers. Tonight we meet people who have carved out “a particular set of skills.” How does one get into the knife-making business? A former chef fills us in. We meet local women bringing high-fashion to clients’ fingertips through extravagant nail art, and one whose special skill is fitting ballet dancers with their pointe shoes. Finally, a seamstress brings customers to tears with handmade teddy bears dedicated to lost loved ones.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Whether you’re dining out, dressing up, or cheering on the home team, you can go super high-end OR wicked wallet-friendly. Tonight we do both! We try the delicious fare at a local high school restaurant…before hitting a nine-course menu at Somerville’s Tasting Counter. We check out a new luxury option for watching a game inside Boston Garden, while across the street at The Harp find affordable draft beer and plenty of TVs. And if you’re a shoe lover? We meet a gifted local artisan who can make you a pricey custom pair… and a former professional wrestler-turned-cobbler who instead can save your sole.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Dining out, dressing up, or planning a wedding, you can go high-end OR wallet-friendly. Tonight, we do both. We sample the innovative fare at high-end JiangNan Boston…then check out the daily specials at a local spot, Mandarin Cuisine.

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: Wellness Hour or Get Fixed

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Clean, categorize, purge… scrub, sort, and sage! Tonight we meet people who were built for spring cleaning.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Scanned the real estate listings lately? Inventory is low, prices are sky-high, and some buyers are going to extreme lengths to lock in a purchase. Tonight Shayna Seymour surveys the market, touring homes at the median price in Greater Boston (hint: it’s high!). We meet a Worcester mom who was forced to widen her search to find an affordable home, and families choosing to remodel rather than compete with other buyers. A local home inspector shares a few hints (and horror stories). Finally, if you’re just dreaming, Shayna gets a peek inside a $7 million mansion that offers a taste of the celebrity lifestyle.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Anthony Everett makes a winter visit to the serene lake and surrounding communities that helped inspire the Oscar-winning film, On Golden Pond. Tonight Anthony chats with the film’s screenwriter, who still calls this region home, and meets a couple who left sunny San Diego to run a Holderness bed-and-breakfast.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Sick of the snow? We have the perfect solution. Tonight, join Anthony Everett as he heads south to explore the clear waters and stunning beaches on St. John in the U.S. Virgin Islands.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Shayna Seymour ventures to Stonington, Connecticut, voted one of the prettiest coastal towns in New England – taking in the salt air on a harbor cruise and hitting a local winery. Ted Reinstein takes a drive to East Greenwich, Rhode Island, where his adventures take him from the town’s historic Main Street to a sailing adventure on Greenwich Bay. And Anthony Everett heads north, visiting South Hero, Vermont, where he checks out a dairy farm that has added wine to its repertoire and bikes to an eatery popular with local foodies.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Ted Reinstein meets local growers who are big on blooms. Their peonies and poppies, sweet peas and snapdragons, lift the mood and beautify the landscape. Among the growers: a physician with a side gig as a lavender farmer, a prominent architect who invites Ted into his secret garden on Beacon Hill, and a small-scale flower grower committed to sustainable practices.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: We’re back with another round of stories suggested by you – our Chronicle viewers.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: You call us, you email us, you load up our social media pages. So tonight we’re making YOU our associate producers with a collection of stories suggested by viewers.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: From the Boston Tea Party – where tea became a symbol of defiance and independence – to elegant afternoon teas. Tonight we are steeped in tea stories!

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: We’re back with another round of stories suggested by you, our viewers!

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: Shayna Seymour heads north to the charming town of Stowe, nestled at the foot of Mount Mansfield, where she explores bucolic farms, a network of mountain biking trails, and shares a brew on the Vermont Beer Trail (with a member of the Von Trapp family). Shayna meets a local family that has transformed a former ski dorm into a cafe and antiques shop. At Percy Farm, a farming family is dedicated to rebuilding after a devastating fire. And is there a better way to spend a few hours than frolicking with golden retrievers? In Stowe, you can do just that.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: Finding Adventure
programme.desc: Anthony Carignan is a bookish 26-year-old who has never felt at ease in the outdoors. Kinga has come to Maine to help Anthony connect with nature and live out his dream of becoming an authentic Mainer.

programme.title: Ed & Day in The 'Burgh
programme.desc: Ed and Day try their hand at pottery and make pretzels in a 150 year old oven before toasting their day at Carson Street Deli & Craft Beer Bar in the Southside.

programme.title: Eat Play Stay
programme.desc: Near the foothills of the Blue Ridge Mountains, go for a spin on the track and on the dance floor. Order a tiny coffee and get cozy in a tiny house. Chat up alpacas and introduce yourself to the granddaughter of a legendary four-legged athlete. It’s all possible in Greer, South Carolina.

programme.desc: On December 10, 2021, a violent tornado outbreak hit western Kentucky. It was the deadliest weather event in the history of the Commonwealth. But as families faced a bleak holiday season, hope emerged in surprising ways.

programme.title: The Spark
programme.desc: From professional hockey players to award-winning muralists, The Spark highlights the craft, the journey, and the joy. These masters and thought leaders may have made names for themselves on the national stage, but they never forget the city that sparks their inspiration daily

programme.title: The Freedom Center
programme.desc: Across centuries of struggle, freedom has carried many meanings, some painful, some powerful. This documentary pulls back the curtain on forgotten stories and surprising truths, leaving one question: what does freedom really look like today, and what might it become tomorrow?

programme.title: Blind Kitchen
programme.desc: Chefs recreate each other’s signature dish, but they can only determine what the dish is using their senses of taste, touch and smell. When the masks come off, the heat is on, with each chef attempting to cook their opponent’s creation.

programme.title: Eat Play Stay
programme.desc: Experience serious vacation vibes and discover some of the most special corners of America on this guided tour of must-visit cities.

programme.title: On The Job
programme.desc: Orlando expert, Brendan O'Connor, celebrates the people behind the scenes of the businesses that make Orlando a truly unique place. Join him as he rolls up his sleeves and receives hands on training while spending a day with them, On The Job.

programme.title: Hit Me With Your Best Spot
programme.desc: No one knows a city like the locals who live there. This series taps into the insider knowledge of notable figures including musicians, athletes, chefs, artists, influencers, and others who share their best spots to hit up in the city.

programme.title: Ballin' in Baltimore
programme.desc: Join WNBA player and Olympian Angel McCoughtry as she shows us the people and places that put Baltimore on the map. From sailing the Chesapeake, to sampling street foods, McCoughtry pulls back the curtain on what makes the Charm City so special.

programme.title: Local Love
programme.desc: One local single experiences some of the best their city has to offer on three blind dates. Our singles may spend three perfect days out on the town, but there can only be one love match. Who will be picked for a second date?

programme.title: Plate It!
programme.desc: Watch things heat up in the kitchen as two local chefs go head-to-head to create a three-course meal utilizing ingredients sourced from one of the city’s unique marketplaces.

programme.desc: Experience serious vacation vibes and discover some of the most special corners of America on this guided tour of must-visit cities.

programme.title: Wait, What Happened?
programme.desc: In this game show, comedian Frank Nicotero hits the streets to ask people questions inspired by the wildest news stories for a chance at winning a cash prize. These real-life stories with unreal outcomes will make you say, “Wait, What Happened?"

programme.title: Stitch
programme.desc: Stitch showcases the feel-good and do-good that make up the fabric of America from inspiring tales of perseverance, to good Samaritans helping neighbors, to trailblazers improving their communities.
